title: The Ultimate Guide to Custom Silverado Emblem Upgrades (2026)
excerpt: Transform your truckâ€™s identity. Discover why hand-engraved Chevrolet emblems are the premium choice for Silverado owners looking to stand out from the factory crowd.
content:
Why Your Silverado Deserves More Than Factory Plastic

The Chevrolet Silverado isnâ€™t just a truckâ€”itâ€™s an American icon of power and reliability. But when you pull into a parking lot or a truck meet, seeing the same factory-standard gold bowtie on every other vehicle can make yours feel like part of the background. Thatâ€™s where custom engraving transforms your build from 'standard' to 'extraordinary'.

Engraving vs. Vinyl Overlays

Many owners start with cheap vinyl overlays or plastic covers. While these are affordable, they often peel, fade, or look 'stuck on' after a single season of rain and car washes. Engraving Nation takes a different approach. By precision-etching deep patterns directly into premium materials, we create a dimensional look that captures light and shadow in ways vinyl simply canâ€™t match.

The Best Styles for 2026 Builds

  • Midnight Honeycomb: A deep black finish with geometric hex patterns. Perfect for 'Blacked Out' Silverado builds.
  • Traditional Scrollwork: For the enthusiast who appreciates classic craftsmanship and hand-drawn artistry.
  • Shattered Carbon: A modern, aggressive look that mimics forged carbon fiber through precision etching.

Fitment Guide: 2007-2024 Models

Whether you drive a classic GMT900 or the latest 2024 ZR2, knowing your emblem size is critical. Most Silverado bowties are either snap-in or bolt-on. At Engraving Nation, we provide exact factory-replacement shells so your install is as clean as the day it left the assembly line.

Pro Tip: Caring for Your Engraved Parts

Because our work is etched into the material, maintenance is simple: wash with standard pH-neutral soap and avoid harsh abrasives. For more inspiration on how these parts look on finished builds, check out our Build Showcase.

title: History of Automotive Engraving: From Hot Rods to Luxury Trucks
excerpt: Engraving isn't new; it's a heritage craft. We trace the lineage of hand-etched metal from the early Lowriders to today's high-end performance trucks.
content:
A Craft as Old as the Industry

Long before CNC machines and lasers, master engravers were adding flair to the first luxury automobiles. In the 1960s and 70s, the Lowrider community in East LA turned engraving into an art form, etching every square inch of chrome on their '64 Impalas.

The Modern Evolution

Today, we've taken that traditional hand-eye coordination and applied it to the aggressive, clean geometric shapes of modern 4x4s. It's a bridge between the soul of the past and the technology of the future.

title: How to Install Your Engraved Chevy Emblem: A Step-by-Step Guide
excerpt: Don't risk damaging your grille. Our master engravers walk you through the precise process of removing factory bowties and installing your custom Engraving Nation masterpiece.
content:
Professional Results Require Professional Preparation

Youâ€™ve invested in a hand-engraved work of art from Engraving Nation. Now, ensuring it sits perfectly on your truck is the final step in the transformation. Whether you're upgrading a classic Silverado or a brand-new 2024 Tahoe, the installation process determines the longevity and 'factory-plus' look of your build.

Tools You'll Need

  • Plastic Trim Removal Tools: Essential for prying without scratching your paint or plastic grille.
  • Heat Gun or Hair Dryer: (For adhesive-backed emblems) to soften the 3M tape.
  • Microfiber Cloths: For cleaning the mounting surface.
  • Isopropyl Alcohol (70%+): To ensure a grease-free bond for the new adhesive.
  • Painter's Tape: To mark alignment and protect surrounding areas.

Step 1: Removal of the Factory Emblem

Most modern Chevy bowties are held in by either plastic clips (snap-in) or automotive adhesive. For clip-style grilles, gently insert your trim tool behind the edge of the emblem and work your way around until you hear the 'click' of the clips releasing. For adhesive-backed badges, apply low heat for 30 seconds to soften the glue, then use a sawing motion with fishing line or a plastic pry tool to lift the emblem.

Step 2: Surface Preparation (The Most Important Step)

Once the old emblem is gone, you'll likely see 'ghosting' from old adhesive or dirt. Use your isopropyl alcohol and a clean microfiber to scrub the mounting area until it is perfectly smooth and tack-free. Any leftover wax or residue will prevent your new engraved emblem from bonding correctly.

Still Haven't Picked Your Design?

Browse our full Chevrolet collection and find your signature pattern.

Shop Chevy Collection

Step 3: Test Fitting & Alignment

Before peeling the adhesive backing off your Engraving Nation emblem, do a 'dry fit'. Note any alignment pins. Use painter's tape on the grille to mark exactly where the top and sides of the emblem should sit. This gives you a visual guide when the adhesive makes contact.

Step 4: The Final Install

Once you are confident in the alignment, peel the 3M backing. Press the emblem firmly into place, starting from the center and moving outward. Apply steady pressure for at least 60 seconds. Pro Tip: Avoid driving or washing your truck for at least 24 hours to allow the adhesive to fully cure.

Need More Help?

If you encounter a unique mounting style or have questions about your specific model year, our team is standing by. Check out our Gallery to see how other owners have positioned their custom badges.
imageAlt: Step-by-step guide on removing a factory Chevrolet bowtie and installing a custom engraved emblem on a Silverado grille.
author: Engraving Nation Team

title: 2019-2024 Silverado Custom Emblem Buyer's Guide
excerpt: Navigating the T1 platform's styling options. From RST to Trail Boss, we break down the best engraved emblem upgrades for the modern Silverado.
content:
Styling the Modern Silverado

The 2019-2024 Chevrolet Silverado (T1 generation) is one of the most technologically advanced trucks on the market. From the factory, these trucks feature bold grilles and distinct trim levels. However, whether you drive a Work Truck or a High Country, standard factory emblems can look repetitive. Custom engraving offers the ultimate personalized bridge between factory and custom.

Why Trim Level Matters

Unlike previous generations, the T1 Silverado uses several distinct grille styles that determine which emblems fit. Understanding your trim is critical:

  • RST and Trail Boss: These trims favor high-contrast 'blacked-out' looks with deep geometric engraving.
  • LTZ and High Country: Owners typically prefer a mix of chrome and custom etching to complement their premium brightwork.

Top Engraved Picks for Modern Silverado Owners

Our artisans recommend matching the engraving pattern to the truck's overall aesthetic:

  • Traditional Scroll for High Country: The refined lines of hand-drawn scrollwork match the luxury interior of the High Country perfectly.
  • Geometric Hex for Z71/Off-Road: If you're hitting the trails, the industrial look of our hex patterns adds a rugged, functional vibe.
  • Complete Bowtie Sets: Most owners opt for matched front and rear sets to ensure a cohesive look across the entire build.

Custom Silverado Emblems

Shop the elite collection of hand-etched badges specifically for the 2019-2024 T1 platform.

Shop Silverado Collection

What About the Tailgate?

The T1 Silverado's multi-flex tailgate and stamped 'CHEVROLET' lettering provide new creative canvases. Instead of just a center bowtie, many owners now add engraved inserts into the stamped letters for a truly 'one-of-one' build.

Durability & Performance

Because our patterns are physically etched into the metal, there are no stickers to peel or paint to chip. These emblems are built to withstand road salt, high-pressure car washes, and UV exposure. A simple seasonal wax is all you need to keep them looking showroom-fresh.

Ready to Start Your Build?

Download our Fitment Guide to confirm your exact sizes before ordering, or check out our Gallery to see real-world T1 builds.

title: Engraving vs. Vinyl Overlays: Which Lasts Longer?
excerpt: Don't waste money on temporary fixes. We compare the lifespan, look, and ROI of permanent engraved emblems vs. cheap vinyl overlays.
content:
The Price of 'Cheap' vs. Premium Craftsmanship

When you're building a truck, the temptation to save money on 'small' details like emblems is real. But after a few years of high-speed highway wind, UV rays, and road salt, the true cost of 'cheap' becomes clear. At Engraving Nation, we consistently see customers who come to us 12 months after buying vinyl overlaysâ€”frustrated by peeling corners and faded colors.

The Longevity Deep-Dive

We've broken down the two most popular customization methods into three key durability metrics:

  • UV Exposure: Vinyl is essentially a sticker. Over time, the sun's rays break down the polymers, leading to color shift (black turns to grey) and brittleness. Engraving is physically etched into the material; metal doesn't 'fade'.
  • Chemical Resistance: Overlays can be damaged by aggressive car wash soaps or road de-icing chemicals. Our engraved emblems are finished with industrial-grade clear coats that resist corrosion.
  • Texture & Feel: An overlay is flat. Our Signature Engraving has actual depth you can feel, catching light dynamically and adding a 'third dimension' that no sticker can mimic.

Invest in Permanence

Explore the durable, hand-etched Chevrolet bowtie collection built for the long haul.

Shop Chevy Collection

Cost-Benefit Analysis: The ROI of Quality

A vinyl overlay might cost $20-$40, while a set of custom engraved emblems is a premium investment. However, if you keep your truck for 5 years, you might replace that vinyl 3 times. More importantly, when it comes time to sell your truck, a 'peeling badge' signals poor maintenance. A set of precision-engraved emblems signals a high-end, well-cared-for build.

The Verdict

If you're looking for a 2-week fix for a car show, vinyl works. But if you're building a truck to lead the pack on the trails or the street for years to come, Engraving is the only choice that matches the heritage of the machine.

Want to See the Difference?

Check out our Build Showcase to see how our emblems look after years of hard work in the field.
imageAlt: Comparison of a peeling vinyl truck overlay vs a sharp, deep-etched hand-engraved Chevrolet emblem showing superior durability and detail.

The Ford Super Duty (F-250, F-350, F-450) is more than just a truck; it's a tool for the most demanding jobs. But for many owners, the factory blue oval can feel a bit predictable. Whether you're hauling a 5th wheel or building a custom show truck, a hand-engraved aluminum emblem adds a layer of sophistication that factory plastic simply cannot match.

Ford trucks use high-grade aluminum in their body construction, and our custom emblems honor that heritage. We use aircraft-grade 6061 billet aluminum, which is significantly more durable than the stock plastic shells. These emblems won't crack under heavy vibration or extreme weather conditions.

  • Corrosion Resistance: Perfect for trucks working in coastal or snowy environments.

Custom Ford Collection

Shop Ford Collection

Our Midnight Honeycomb or Shattered Carbon engraved ovals provide a high-end metallic alternative that maintains the stealth look while adding deep texture and detail.

The Verdict

Your Super Duty was built to stand out. Don't let the factory badges hold back your build. An investment in custom engraving is an investment in the identity of your machine.

title: How to Measure Your Truck Emblem: A Visual Guide
excerpt: Avoid the return shipping hassle. Learn the precise way to measure your Chevrolet, GMC, or Ford emblem to ensure a perfect fit the first time.
content:
Stop Guessing, Start Measuring

One of the most common mistakes truck owners make is assuming all 'Silverado' or 'F-150' emblems are the same size across different years or trim levels. In reality, Chevrolet alone has used over 10 different bowtie sizes in the last decade. Measuring correctly is the only way to guarantee a seamless, factory-plus installation.

The Three Critical Measurements

When measuring your factory emblem, you need to be precise. Use a digital caliper if possible, but a standard tape measure will work if used carefully:

  • Maximum Width (W): Measure from the furthest left point to the furthest right point.
  • Maximum Height (H): Measure from the top-most point to the bottom-most point.
  • Depth/Thickness (D): This is crucial for recessed grilles where the emblem must 'seat' properly.

Common Pitfalls to Avoid

Do not measure the *recess* in your grille; always measure the **existing emblem shell**. Many grilles have a hidden border that can mislead your measurements by as much as 1/4 inch.

Still Unsure?

Check out our technical fitment guides for specific Chevy and GMC model years.

View Technical Guide

Year-Specific Warnings

Be aware that refresh years (like the 2022.5 Silverado) often transition between different mounting styles. If your truck was manufactured in a transition year, always check if your emblem is 'pushed-in' (clips) or 'stuck-on' (adhesive) before ordering.
imageAlt: A technician using digital calipers to measure a Chevrolet bowtie emblem height and width for precision custom fitment.
author: Engraving Nation Team

seoMetadata.title: How to Measure Truck Emblems | Fitment Guide | Engraving Nation
seoMetadata.description: The ultimate technical guide for measuring Chevrolet, GMC, and Ford emblems. Ensure a perfect fit for your custom hand-engraved badge.

title: Complete Color Matching Guide for Custom Emblems
excerpt: Get that 'factory-plus' look. We break down common GM, Ford, and RAM paint codes and how we match our custom engravings to your truck's finish.
content:
The Science of a Seamless Finish

For many truck enthusiasts, the goal is 'OEM+': a build that looks like it could have come from the factory, but with a level of detail that factory machines can't achieve. The secret to this look is precision color matching. At Engraving Nation, we don't just use 'close enough' colors; we work with actual automotive paint codes to ensure your custom parts integrate perfectly with your truck's bodywork.

Understanding Your Paint Code

Before ordering your color-matched emblems, you need to find your truck's specific paint code. These are typically found on the driver-side door jamb sticker or inside the glove box. Common codes we work with include:

  • GM Summit White (GBA/8624): A crisp, clean white used on Silverados and Sierras for decades.
  • Ford Shadow Black (G1): A deep, non-metallic black that provides the perfect contrast for raw aluminum engraving.
  • RAM Flame Red (PR4): A bold, high-visibility red that demands an aggressive etched pattern.

Request a Custom Match

Don't see your code? Contact our design team for a bespoke color-matching consultation.

Inquire About Custom Paint

The 'Contrast' Strategy

Sometimes, the best look isn't an exact match, but a calculated contrast. For example, a Midnight Honeycomb emblem on a Quicksilver Metallic truck creates a sophisticated, high-tech aesthetic that draws the eye without being 'loud'.

Professional Clear-Coating

Every color-matched piece we produce is finished with a multi-stage, high-UV automotive clear coat. This is the same grade used on professional body shop repairs, ensuring your emblems won't yellow, peel, or fade under the sun.
imageAlt: A row of custom engraved Chevrolet bowties painted in various factory-match colors like Summit White, Black, and Silver Ice Metallic.
author: Engraving Nation Team

seoMetadata.title: Truck Emblem Color Matching Guide | Engraving Nation
seoMetadata.description: Learn how to find your truck's paint code and achieve a perfect color match for your custom engraved emblems. Covering GM, Ford, and RAM codes.

title: Why Hand-Engraving Outlasts Machine-Etching
excerpt: It's all in the depth. We explain the technical differences between shallow laser etching and the deep, permanent signature of hand-craftsmanship.
content:
The Depth of Detail

In the world of customization, the word 'engraving' is often used loosely. Many mass-market retailers use low-power lasers to 'etch' a design onto the surface of a plastic part. At Engraving Nation, we do things differently. We use a physical, hand-guided process that cuts deep into the material, creating a permanent signature that no machine can replicate.

The Technical Difference

Machine Etching (Laser/CNC): This process typically only disrupts the top 0.001 to 0.005 inches of the material. While it looks clean initially, road debris and high-pressure car washes can slowly 'errase' the detail over time.

Signature Hand-Engraving: Our process cuts as deep as 0.020 to 0.040 inches. This creates actual physical geometry that catches light and shadow dynamically. Because the design is part of the material's structure, it is physically impossible to 'wear off'.

  • Artistic Soul: No two hand-engraved pieces are identical. You are buying a 'one-of-one' piece of art for your vehicle.
  • Durability: Hand-cut grooves provide a better surface for paint and clear-coat adhesion.

The Craftsmanship Video

Watch our master engravers at work on a limited-edition 'Traditional Scroll' bowtie.

Watch the Craft

The ROI of Heritage

While hand-engraving takes longer and requires a higher initial investment, it is the only method that lasts the lifetime of the vehicle. When you see a truck at a show with 'faded' laser etching, you'll understand why our customers choose the permanent path.
imageAlt: A macro shot comparing the deep, shaded cuts of a hand-engraved emblem vs the flat, shallow look of a laser-etched badge.
author: Engraving Nation Team

seoMetadata.title: Hand-Engraved vs. Machine-Etched Emblems | Engraving Nation
seoMetadata.description: Discover the technical and artistic differences between hand-engraving and machine etching. Learn why deep-cut craftsmanship is the superior choice.

title: Mirror Cap Installation: Everything You Need to Know
excerpt: Complete your build. Learn how to safely remove factory mirror caps and install our custom hand-engraved aluminum replacements.
content:
The Final Detail: Mirror Caps

Once you've upgraded your front and rear emblems, the mirrors are the next logical focal point. A set of custom-engraved mirror caps balances the profile of the truck, creating a cohesive design language from nose to tail. Today, we're walking through the installation process for the most common T1 Silverado/Sierra (2019+) and Ford F-Series mirrors.

Preparation is Key

Before you begin, ensure you have a set of **plastic trim tools**. Never use a metal screwdriver, as you will inevitably mar the plastic mirror housing or glass. For many models, you will also need to adjust the mirror glass fully inward to access the internal clips.

  • Safety Tip: Wear gloves. Mirror glass can be fragile, and some models have integrated wiring for blind-spot monitors.
  • Temperature: Never attempt this in freezing weather; cold plastic clips are prone to snapping. Install in a warm garage or on a sunny day.

Shop Custom Mirror Caps

Complete your build with our matching honeycomb or scroll-work cap sets.

Browse Mirror Caps

The 'Pop and Click' Method

Most modern mirror caps are held in by 4-6 large plastic clips. Work your trim tool around the seam, applying steady, outward pressure. You will hear a series of loud 'pops'â€”this is normal. Once the factory cap is off, simply align our custom aluminum cap and apply firm pressure until it 'clicks' into place.

Verification

Ensure the mirror glass moves freely through its full range of motion. If you have integrated turn signals, verify they are visible and not obstructed by the new cap.
imageAlt: A step-by-step visual of a technician installing a custom hand-engraved mirror cap on a 2024 Chevrolet Silverado.
author: Engraving Nation Team

seoMetadata.title: Mirror Cap Installation Guide | Truck Customization | Engraving Nation
seoMetadata.description: Learn the safe and correct way to install custom engraved mirror caps on your truck. Step-by-step guide for Chevy, GMC, and Ford models.

title: SEMA 2026: Top Trends in Custom Truck Accessories
excerpt: A glimpse into the future. We preview the dominant engineering and styling trends from the world's biggest automotive trade show.
content:
What's Next for the Truck Industry?

As we look ahead to **SEMA 2026**, the industry is shifting from 'loud and flashy' to 'functional and refined'. The 'Mall Crawler' era is giving way to high-end, purpose-built machines that prioritize engineering and material honesty. At Engraving Nation, we're already integrating these trends into our latest releases.

Dominant Trend 1: Material Authenticity

Plastic is out; raw metal and carbon fiber are in. Builders are looking for parts that have physical weight and substance. This is why our **Signature Series Billet Aluminum** line has seen record demandâ€”owners want to feel the quality every time they walk up to their truck.

Dominant Trend 2: Integrated Lighting

We're seeing a massive surge in 'Glow Emblems' that use subtle, diffused LED lighting to highlight the engraving detail at night. While we currently focus on pure metalwork, we are exploring ways to incorporate backlighting into our **Shadow-Engraved** series for a futuristic look.

Join the Inner Circle

Sign up for our newsletter to get early access to our SEMA-inspired 2026 limited drop.

Join VIP List

The 'Electric' Revolution (EV Emblems)

With the rise of the **Silverado EV** and **F-150 Lightning**, the canvas for custom badges is changing. These grilles are often solid, flat panels, allowing for much larger, more intricate engravings. We are developing specific patterns that complement the aerodynamic styling of the new electric fleet.

Stay Ahead of the Pack

The best way to ensure your build stays relevant is to invest in timeless craftsmanship rather than passing fads. Hand-engraved art is one of the few modifications that looks better as the vehicle ages.
imageAlt: A futuristic truck build at SEMA featuring integrated lighting and ultra-detailed metal engraving on the body and badges.
author: Engraving Nation Team

seoMetadata.title: SEMA 2026 Custom Truck Trends | Future Outlook | Engraving Nation
seoMetadata.description: What to expect from the custom truck industry in 2026. From material honesty to EV-specific engraving, stay ahead of the SEMA trends.

title: 2026 GMC Sierra Denali Customization Guide: Why Black Chrome Engraving is the New Standard
excerpt: The Denali name represents the peak of GMC luxury. Discover how hand-engraved black chrome accents are redefining the 'Professional Grade' aesthetic for the 2026 Sierra.
content:
Elevating the 'Professional Grade' Standard

The GMC Sierra Denali has long been the benchmark for luxury trucks. Its signature chrome grille and refined interior set it apart from the workhorse crowd. However, in 2026, the trend is shifting away from 'flashy' chrome toward 'refined character'. Hand-engraved black chrome is the emerging standard for owners who want their Denali to be truly one-of-one.

The Appeal of Black Chrome Engraving

Standard bright chrome can sometimes feel overwhelming, especially on the massive Denali grille. Black Chrome provides a deeper, more sophisticated tone that retains a metallic lustre while adding a 'stealth' quality. When our master artisans etch signature patterns like Midnight Honeycomb or Traditional Scroll into these surfaces, the result is a dynamic interplay of light and shadow that bright chrome simply cannot achieve.

Top 3 Customization Trends for 2026 Denali Owners

  • Ghost-Etched Logos: Replacing the bright red GMC logo with a color-matched black chrome emblem that features deep-etched 'ghost' patterns.
  • Engraved Mirror Caps: Extending the design language to the side profile with matched patterns on the mirror housings.
  • Satin-Contrast Accents: Intermixing satin black finishes with polished engraving for a multi-textured, high-end look.

Dominate the Denali Scene

Explore our exclusive GMC Denali collection and start your transformation today.

Shop GMC Collection

Fitment & Durability

The 2026 Sierra platform continues the legacy of precision engineering. Our custom engraved parts are designed as direct factory replacements, ensuring a perfect fit without the need for drilling or permanent modifications. Because we etch directly into high-grade aluminum and finish with automotive-strength clear coats, your custom accents will withstand road salt, high-pressure washes, and years of sunlight.

Why Choose Engraving Over Vinyl?

While vinyl 'chrome deletes' are popular, they lack the physical depth and permanence required for a vehicle of this caliber. Engraving is a heritage craftâ€”it is a physical transformation of the material that signals a commitment to quality and longevity. On a Denali, 'temporary' fixes like stickers decrease the perceived value of the build.

Conclusion

Your Sierra Denali was built to be the best. By incorporating hand-engraved black chrome accents, you aren't just following a trend; you're setting a new standard for what 'Professional Grade' really means in 2026.

For more inspiration on how these parts look on finished builds, check out our Build Showcase and see how other Denali owners are leading the pack.
imageAlt: A 2026 GMC Sierra Denali with custom hand-engraved black chrome emblems and mirror caps in a high-luxury setting.

title: How to Remove Chevy Emblems: 2014-2018 Silverado (K2XX)
excerpt: A comprehensive guide on safely removing the front and rear emblems from 2014-2018 Silverado trucks without damaging the paint or grille.
content:
Clean Removal for the K2XX Generation

The 2014-2018 Chevrolet Silverado (K2XX) is known for its bold, chrome-heavy front end. Whether you're upgrading to a hand-engraved masterpiece or simply blacking out your build, removing the factory bowtie is the first step.

Tools Needed

  • Fishing line or dental floss
  • Heat gun or hairdryer
  • Adhesive remover (like Goo Gone)
  • Plastic pry tools
  • Microfiber cloth

Step-By-Step: Tailgate Emblem Removal

Unlike the grille, the tailgate emblem is held on strictly by high-strength adhesive. Heat the emblem for 60 seconds, then use length of fishing line to 'saw' through the foam tape behind the badge. Once the badge is off, apply adhesive remover to the residue and gently wipe away.

Upgrade Your Look

Shop Engraved Bowties

title: Safe Grille Emblem Removal for 2015-2017 Chevy Silverado
excerpt: Learn how to access the clips behind the grille of your 2015-2017 Silverado to remove the front bowtie without snapping the plastic tabs.
content:
Don't Snap Those Clips

Removing the front emblem on a 2015-2017 Silverado can be frustrating because it's held by mechanical clips, not just glue. If you pull too hard from the front, you risk cracking the grille housing.

Accessing the Back of the Grille

Open the hood and remove the plastic radiator shroud (held by 10-12 plastic push pins). This allows you to reach down behind the grille. Locate the two primary tabs holding the bowtie. Press them inward while another person gently pulls the emblem from the front.

Pro Tip: Work in the Sun

Plastic clips are more brittle in the cold. Doing this on a warm day or in a heated garage ensures the tabs flex instead of snapping.

Looking for a Replacement?

View Custom Grille Emblems

title: Silverado 2500 Tailgate Emblem Removal: Step-by-Step
excerpt: The heavy-duty 2500 models feature larger tailgate badges. Here is how to handle the extra surface area and ensure a clean finish.
content:
Handling HD Tailgate Badges

The Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD features massive tailgate branding. When removing these for a custom Engraving Nation set, you need to account for the larger adhesive surface area.

Step 1: Outlining

Use blue painter's tape to mark the exact location of the original badges. This ensures your new custom emblems are perfectly aligned when you install them later.

Step 2: The Fishing Line Method

Because the 2500HD badges are thicker, use a heavy-duty 20lb test fishing line. Work from the corners inward, keeping the line as close to the emblem back as possible to avoid scratching the clear coat.

HD Series Customization

excerpt: Upgrading your Z71? Here is how to mount the specialized grille emblem on the new T1 generation Silverado trucks.
content:
Upgrade Your Z71 Identity

The T1 Silverado (2019+) features a more complex grille structure. If you've purchased a custom-etched Z71 emblem from Engraving Nation, here is how to ensure a factory-tight fit.

Preparation

Most 2019-2022 Z71 grille emblems use a back-plate and screw system or a heavy-duty compression clip. You may need to remove the top radiator cover to get enough hand clearance to reach the mounting point.

Alignment

The Z71 badge has a specific 'seat' in the mesh of the grille. Ensure it is perfectly level before tightening the nuts or snapping the clips. A crooked Z71 badge is the hallmark of a rushed build!

Z71 Precision Gear

title: Blacking Out Your 2015 Silverado Grill: Painting vs. Vinyl
excerpt: A detailed comparison of the best ways to achieve the 'Murdered Out' look on your 2015 Silverado's front end.
content:
The Quest for the Stealth Look

The 2015 Silverado is a favorite for 'Stealth' or 'Murdered Out' builds. But should you paint your grille or use a vinyl wrap? Or is there a third, more permanent option?

Painting (Permanent)

Painting provides the best color match and gloss level but is prone to rock chips on the highway. Once it chips, the original chrome or plastic shows through, looking unsightly.

Vinyl Wrap (Temporary)

Vinyl is great for experimentation but lacks the depth of high-end finishes. In the heat of summer, vinyl on a front grille can begin to peel at the edges due to engine heat.

The Engraving Nation Solution

Why compromise? Our fully engraved black emblems provide the depth of custom art with the durability of heavy-duty materials that won't chip like paint or peel like vinyl.

Beyond Paint and Vinyl

excerpt: Technical specs and measurements for the classic GMT800 Silverado tailgate badges to ensure perfect replacement sizing.
content:
Classic Sizing for the GMT800

The 1999-2006 (and 2007 Classic) Silverado remains one of the most popular trucks for restoration. If you are replacing your worn badges with custom-engraved ones, getting the measurements right is key.

Standard Dimensions

  • Full Size Bowtie: Approximately 9.5 inches wide.
  • 'Silverado' Script: Varies by year, typically 12 inches in length.
  • 'LT'/'LS' Badges: Standardized 2x3 inch blocks.

At Engraving Nation, we ensure our replacement sets match these factory silhouettes perfectly while adding the intricate scrollwork that makes your classic truck stand out at the show.

title: Illuminated Chevy Emblem Kits: DIY Install for 2016-2018 Silverado
excerpt: Take your front end to the next level by combining engraving with LED illumination. Here is how to wire up your lighted bowtie.
content:
Light Up the Night

Nothing turns heads like an illuminated front bowtie. When you pair an LED kit with an Engraving Nation custom bowtie, the light catches the etched grooves for a celestial effect that paint simply can't achieve.

Wiring into the Headlight Harness

For a clean install, you'll want to tap into your DRL (Daytime Running Light) circuit. This ensures the emblem glows whenever your truck is running. Use waterproof heat-shrink butt connectors to prevent corrosion—remember, this badge sits right in the path of road spray.

Mounting the LED Housing

The 2016-2018 grille has enough depth to house the LED back-plate. Ensure the wiring is tucked neatly along the radiator support and zip-tied away from moving parts or high-heat areas like the AC condenser.

The Ultimate Night Look

title: Does a Silverado Emblem Fit a Camaro? Fitment Guide
excerpt: Can you swap badges between the bowtie family? We break down which Silverado emblems are compatible with the Chevrolet Camaro.
content:
Cross-Model Compatibility

It's a common question among Chevy enthusiasts: 'Can I put a truck emblem on my muscle car?' While the bowtie is universal, the mounting systems are often very different.

Front Grille Fitment

Silverado emblems are significantly larger and deeper than Camaro emblems. Attempting to fit a 2014 Silverado front bowtie on a 6th Gen Camaro will result in a badge that overflows the mounting pocket.

Tailgate vs. Trunk

The *rear* tailgate bowties from the 1999-2006 Silverado are flat and adhesive-backed, making them potentially compatible with the flat surfaces of older Camaro trunks. However, modern Camaros have curved trunk lids that require specific contoured emblems.

Custom Fit for Every Model

title: How to Remove & Replace Door Badges on a 2014+ Chevy Silverado
excerpt: Clean up the side profile of your truck. Learn how to remove the 'Silverado' and trim level badges from your doors without leaving ghosting.
content:
Debadging for a Cleaner Profile

Side door badges contribute to a lot of 'visual clutter.' Many owners choose to remove them or replace them with smaller, more subtle custom-engraved pieces from Engraving Nation.

Dealing with Ghosting

Older trucks (5+ years) may experience 'ghosting' where the paint under the emblem hasn't faded like the rest of the door. To fix this, you'll need a light polish and a DA polisher to blend the surfaces once the adhesive is gone.

Installing Custom Side Badges

If you're replacing the stock text with a custom 2500HD or High Country engraved plate, use a level and a laser line to ensure the badges are identical on both the driver and passenger sides.

title: Where is the G80 Emblem Located? Identifying Your Silverado's Diff
excerpt: Looking for the G80 code or emblem? We help you locate the identification marks for the legendary locking differential on your Chevy truck.
content:
The Myth of the G80 'Emblem'

Unlike the Z71 or Trail Boss, the **G80 Locking Differential** rarely has an external emblem from the factory. Most enthusiasts add their own custom G80 badges to signal that their truck is truly off-road capable.

Finding the Evidence

To confirm your truck has a G80 locker, check the glove box for a sticker with the 'RPO' (Regular Production Option) codes. If you see 'G80' in that alphabet soup, you have the mechanical locker.

Adding a Custom G80 Badge

If you're proud of your truck's capability, a hand-engraved G80 emblem on the tailgate or fender is the perfect subtle modification. Our G80 series features rugged, industrial patterns that match the heavy-duty nature of the locker itself.

Represent the G80

title: Plasti-Dip Guide: Easy DIY Blackout for Silverado Badges
excerpt: The beginner's path to blacking out emblems. Learn the pros and cons of Plasti-Dip compared to high-end engraved replacements.
content:
The Gateway Mod

Plasti-Dip is often the first modification a new truck owner attempts. It's cheap, removable, and provides an instant 'blackout' look. But is it right for a premium build?

The Process

Mask off a rectangular area around the emblem, leaving about 2 inches of 'overspray' area. Apply 5-6 very thin coats, waiting 10 minutes between each. Once dry, peel away the excess, and the dip should naturally separate at the edges of the emblem.

The Longevity Issue

Plasti-Dip is a rubberized coating. Over time, road salt and high-pressure car washes will cause it to tear. For owners who want a permanent, luxury finish that doesn't feel like rubber, custom-etched metal from Engraving Nation is the natural next step.

Time to Level Up?

Upgrade to Engraved Metal

title: Restoration Corner: 77-87 Silverado Bowtie Grille Attachment
excerpt: Classic Squarebody restoration. Learn how the emblems attached to the iconic 1977-1987 Chevrolet Silverado grilles.
content:
Squarebody Archeology

The 1977-1987 'Squarebody' Chevrolet trucks are legendary. Restoring one requires an eye for detail, especially when it comes to the front grille bowtie which changed mounting styles almost every year.

Screw-Through vs. Snap-In

Early Squarebodies often used a screw-through-the-grille mounting system, with the bowtie sitting on a specific plastic bracket. By the mid-80s, these transitioned to plastic nuts on the back of the grille.

Adding Custom Flair to a Classic

A restoration doesn't have to be 100% factory. A custom-etched bowtie that follows the classic 1980s geometric patterns can add a 'Restomod' feel that separates your truck from every other C10 at the cruise-in.

Classic Heritage Customization

title: Restoring the 1999 Classic Silverado: A Badge-by-Badge Guide
excerpt: Take your GMT800 restoration to the next level. We break down the exact badges needed to bring your classic 1999 Silverado back to showroom glory.
content:
The Golden Era of Chevy Trucks

The 1999 Chevrolet Silverado marked the beginning of the legendary GMT800 platform. Today, these trucks have crossed from 'used workhorses' into highly sought-after restoration projects. While tracking down heavy mechanical parts is easy, getting the aesthetics right requires attention to the details—specifically, the badges.

The Front Bowtie: Classic Proportions

The original 1999 grille featured a prominent bowtie measuring roughly 10.25 inches. Many enthusiasts find that factory plastic replacements crack easily. A custom-engraved aluminum bowtie in a polished or traditional scroll finish provides a durable, OEM+ look that respects the truck's heritage.

Tailgate Identity

The tailgate of the '99 used an iconic adhesive-backed 'Silverado' script alongside the rear bowtie. When replacing these, careful surface preparation is key. Use adhesive remover to clear 25 years of ghosting before applying your new custom aluminum pieces.

Restore Your GMT800

title: Why Aluminum Billets Outlast Plastic in Salt-Road States
excerpt: Winter is cruel to custom trucks. Discover why upgrading to deep-etched aluminum billets is the only way to beat rust belt corrosion.
content:
The Rust Belt Reality

If you live in the Midwest or Northeast, you know the devastation road salt and brine cause to vehicles. While we constantly wash our undercarriages, we often forget about the front grille. Factory plastic emblems with chrome flashing are notoriously weak against salt-induced pitting and peeling.

The Science of Billet Aluminum

Unlike cheap plastic that degrades under combined UV and chemical stress, we carve our custom emblems from solid 6061 billet aluminum. Aluminum naturally forms a protective oxide layer, and when finished with our high-grade automotive clear coats, it becomes virtually impermeable to winter road treatments.

No More Peeling Chrome

There is nothing worse than a factory badge where the fake chrome starts flaking off, leaving white plastic exposed. By investing in solid metal, you guarantee your truck's identity survives the winter looking exactly as it did in July.

Winter-Proof Your Build

Shop Billet Aluminum Upgrades

title: The SEMA 2026 Trend Report: Why Integrated Luxury is Replacing Flashy Builds
excerpt: The era of the loud, bolt-on catalog build is over. We explore the 'Integrated Luxury' movement dominating the 2026 truck customization scene.
content:
The Maturation of Truck Customization

Walk the floor of SEMA in 2026, and you'll immediately notice a shift. Builders are moving away from fluorescent suspension parts and oversized fake-rivet flares. The new undisputed king of the show floor is a concept known as Integrated Luxury.

What is Integrated Luxury?

It's the pursuit of the 'OEM++' aesthetic. Modifications should look like they came from a secret, high-end factory skunkworks division. Instead of bolting on loud accessories, builders are replacing factory components with hyper-detailed, premium material versions.

Deep Engraving as the Centerpiece

This is exactly why hand-engraved metal has surged in popularity. An engraved black chrome Chevy bowtie doesn't scream for attention from three blocks away. Instead, it rewards the viewer when they get close, revealing complex texture, weight, and undeniable craftsmanship. It is the definition of quiet luxury for the modern truck.

Join the Movement

Upgrade to Integrated Luxury

title: Chevy Silverado High Country Emblem Customization: Retaining the Luxury Look
excerpt: The High Country trim demands premium details. Discover how to upgrade your emblems without losing the sophisticated aesthetic of Chevy's top-tier Silverado.
content:
Quick Answer:The best custom emblem upgrade for a Chevy Silverado High Country integrates seamlessly with the factory brightwork. Precision hand-engraved aluminum bowties featuring traditional scrollwork or gold-inlay accents offer the ideal balance, providing a custom aesthetic that matches the High Country's premium luxury trim without looking cheap or overly aggressive.

The High Country Dilemma

Owning a Silverado High Country means you've invested in Chevrolet's pinnacle of luxury and capability. The factory chrome grille is a statement piece. But when it comes to personalization, many aftermarket badges (like matte black plastic overlays) can actually downgrade the truck's premium feel.

Matching the Brightwork

To maintain the luxury aesthetic, custom emblems must complement, not clash with, the existing chrome and bronze accents of the High Country trim. We recommend polished aluminum or black chrome bases.

  • Traditional Scrollwork: The intricate, hand-drawn lines of traditional engraving pair perfectly with the High Country's refined interior stitching and exterior badging.
  • Subtle Depth: Unlike flat vinyl, our deep etching catches the light just like the factory chrome, creating a cohesive visual experience.

title: Silverado ZR2 Blackout Emblem Upgrades: Deep Etching vs. Inserts
excerpt: Take your ZR2's aggressive styling to the next level. We compare standard plastic insert replacements with deep-etched blackout emblem kits.
content:
Quick Answer:For the Silverado ZR2, deep-etched black chrome emblems provide superior durability and styling compared to standard plastic inserts. The physical geometry of the engraving withstands trail debris and harsh conditions, while the 'Midnight Honeycomb' pattern perfectly complements the ZR2's aggressive, off-road focused front fascia.

Enhancing the Apex Predator

The Silverado ZR2 is built to dominate the trails. Its unique flow-tie grille and high-clearance bumper give it a menacing presence. While many owners opt for the 'Blackout' package from the factory, the standard black plastic bowties can lack character.

The Problem with Snap-in Inserts

Many aftermarket companies sell cheap, colored plastic inserts to replace the red "2" in the ZR2 badge or the center of the bowtie. However, the retaining tabs on these inserts are notoriously brittle. One hard hit from a rock or branch on the trail, and you're left with a missing piece.

The Engraving Nation Solution

We replace the entire badge structure with aircraft-grade aluminum. Our Midnight Honeycomb etching matches the rugged nature of the ZR2. The deep cuts not only look aggressive but also hide micro-scratches from off-road use better than flat gloss plastic.

Upgrade Your ZR2

Get the trail-tested durability your truck deserves.

title: The Truth About Automotive Adhesive: Preventing Emblem Failure
excerpt: Why do some aftermarket badges fall off in the car wash? It all comes down to adhesive grading and surface preparation.
content:
Quick Answer:Emblem adhesion failure is usually caused by inadequate surface preparation or the use of non-automotive grade double-sided tape. To ensure permanent installation, the vehicle surface must be cleaned with 70%+ isopropyl alcohol, applied in temperatures around 70°F (21°C), and secured using 3M VHB (Very High Bond) automotive acrylic foam tape.

The Hidden Cost of Cheap Tape

You've just spent good money on a set of custom engraved emblems. The last thing you want is to see them flying off in your rearview mirror on the highway. While the emblem itself is important, the adhesive backing is the unsung hero of the installation.

Not All Tape is Created Equal

Many budget accessories use standard double-sided foam tape. This tape degrades quickly when exposed to UV rays, extreme heat from the engine bay, or freezing winter temperatures. At Engraving Nation, we strictly use 3M VHB (Very High Bond) Automotive Tape.

The Golden Rules of Installation

Even the best tape will fail if the surface isn't prepped correctly. Always follow these rules:

  • Temperature Control: Never install emblems on a freezing cold or blistering hot grille. Aim for 70°F.
  • The Alcohol Wipe: Standard car wash soap leaves behind waxes and gloss-enhancers. You must strip these away using isopropyl alcohol before applying the new emblem.
  • Pressure and Time: Press firmly across the entire surface of the emblem for 60 seconds. The adhesive takes 24-48 hours to fully 'cure' and reach 100% bond strength. Do not wash your truck during this window.
